DONT FLUSH.

Kinda depends if you're on city water or septic. If you're on septic you can still use as normal (if you have power) as long as your septic tank isn't full. However, you should still try not to put much down the drains due to all the rainfall. If it's yellow let it mellow. If it's brown flush it down.
If you're on city water, your county might've shut off the water. In that case, don't flush and avoid putting anything in the drain.
don’t flush. They do this to protect the infrastructure from the saltwater. Saltwater is super destructive. If you do flash and keep doing so things will eventually back up your pipes into your home.

I spent a good amount of time tracking this storm continually watching news reports and checking various weather sites for wind probabilities in my area (Riverview, FL). I just kept hearing that the worst winds would cover a narrow band of maybe 10-15 miles from the eye. It was never mention anywhere that the eye wall would expand to such a size that a 100+ mph swath of wind would ultimately march across all of Hillsborough county, plus several surrounding counties. Currently, at 7:54 EDT, the National Weather Service is showing we will experienced wind gusts of 108 mph at 12 Midnight, despite the Extreme Wind Warning only running through 9:30pm. Perhaps the warning will eventually be extended? Very surprised by this development, to say the least. This must be a very uncommon phenomena.
